name.english: Party of Socialists of the Republic of Moldova

summary: The Party of Action and Solidarity (PAS) is an active centre-right, pro-European political party in Moldova. Founded by Maia Sandu in 2016, it has led the national government since August 2021 and retained a parliamentary majority in the September 2025 election.

history.formation: PAS developed from the civic-political initiative «În PAS cu Maia Sandu», announced by former education minister Maia Sandu on 23 December 2015. Its constituent congress was held on 15 May 2016 and unanimously elected Sandu as party president. The Ministry of Justice formally registered the party and its symbols on 26 May 2016.

labels.name: Social liberalism
labels.note: PAS itself uses the term social liberalism; the European Party Monitor describes its doctrine as New Liberalism, combining a free-market economy with a social welfare state.

labels.name: Pro-Europeanism
labels.note: European Union integration is a central programmatic objective.

labels.name: Anti-corruption
labels.note: Anti-corruption and institutional integrity are central elements of the party programme.

position.label: Centre-right
position.note: This placement is used both by the party and by KU Leuven's European Party Monitor.

leadership.title: President
leadership.note: Re-elected for a new four-year term on 14 June 2026.

governmentStatus.label: government
governmentStatus.description: PAS has led majority governments since the Gavrilița cabinet took office on 6 August 2021. It won 55 of 101 parliamentary mandates in the September 2025 election and supports the government headed by Prime Minister Alexandru Munteanu, sworn in on 1 November 2025.

editorial.note: The logo is currently loaded from Wikimedia Commons. For maximum reliability, download the SVG to public/logos/md-pas.svg and replace the logo value with /logos/md-pas.svg. Exact brand colour hex values were not inferred; the colour field therefore uses the names yellow and black.
